gapsIdentified.title: Cursor-based pagination via pageInfo.endCursor
gapsIdentified.rationale: MKT-GQL-006 uses numeric offset strings (after:'0', after:'2') which are undocumented server behavior. Canonical pagination uses pageInfo.endCursor from the connection type. A regression that breaks cursor-style pagination while keeping offset-style working would be invisible. Context7 confirmed pageInfo {endCursor, hasNextPage} is available on the return type.

gapsIdentified.title: edges[].node / edges[].cursor selection — connection type field coverage
gapsIdentified.rationale: The promotionCoupons query exposes a connection type with edges[] alongside items[]. No test selects edges[].node or edges[].cursor. This is a schema-coverage gap for the edges field but low business value — all practical consumers use items[]. Worth one schema-coverage test but not P1. Defer to a future schema-coverage sprint.

gapsIdentified.title: keyword filter argument — no test covers filtering by keyword
gapsIdentified.rationale: Schema confirms promotionCoupons accepts keyword: String. No test in 050j exercises it. A regression that silently breaks keyword filtering would be invisible. The checklist graphql-checklist.md section 'New Query/Mutation Verification' requires all optional args to be covered at P1 for a new query.
gapsIdentified.suggestedTitle: promotionCoupons — keyword Filter Returns Matching Promotions

gapsIdentified.title: sort argument — no test covers sorting
gapsIdentified.rationale: Schema confirms promotionCoupons accepts sort: String. No test covers it. Lower priority than keyword because sort order correctness is less critical to business logic. Still worth one positive case asserting that results order differs when sort is applied vs default.
gapsIdentified.suggestedTitle: promotionCoupons — sort Argument Changes Result Order

gapsIdentified.title: currencyCode argument — no test exercises currency-specific coupon visibility
gapsIdentified.rationale: Schema accepts currencyCode: String on promotionCoupons. Live verification (2026-04-27) returned same 19 coupons regardless of currency context. The resolver likely does not filter by currency. Low value — defer unless a currency-specific promotion visibility feature is added.

gapsIdentified.title: userId argument — personalized coupon visibility scoping
gapsIdentified.rationale: Schema accepts userId: String. If the resolver scopes coupon visibility to a specific user (e.g., user-targeted promotions), omitting or using a different userId could return wrong coupons. This is a data-isolation concern related to BL-AUTH-005 (RBAC). Needs one test: same store, two user IDs, assert results are user-scoped or identical (documenting the actual behavior as a contract).
gapsIdentified.suggestedTitle: promotionCoupons — userId Argument Scopes Results to Specified User
gapsIdentified.note: May need live verification first — if server returns identical results regardless of userId, the case should document that as expected behavior.

gapsIdentified.title: Coupon usage count exhaustion — promotionCoupons filters out exhausted coupons
gapsIdentified.rationale: BL-PRICE-001 covers discount stacking order but not resolver-level filtering of exhausted coupons. Whether promotionCoupons excludes coupons with maxUsesNumber=0 remaining is a resolver correctness question, but requires Admin data manipulation (setting a coupon to exhausted state). P2 — defer until a dedicated marketing regression sprint.

gapsIdentified.title: Expired coupon date filtering — promotionCoupons excludes past-endDate coupons
gapsIdentified.rationale: Live data showed endDate may be null (open-ended) or a specific date. MKT-GQL-001 asserts endDate is ISO-8601 when present but does NOT assert that past-endDate coupons are excluded from results. A regression that surfaces expired coupons to users would cause confusion (ECL-1.3: coupon expiration pattern). Needs one test: create/identify a promotion with endDate in the past and assert it is absent from promotionCoupons results.
gapsIdentified.suggestedTitle: promotionCoupons — Expired Promotions (endDate in Past) Excluded From Results
gapsIdentified.note: Requires a promotion with endDate in the past to exist in the QA environment — may need seed data.

gapsIdentified.title: E2E coupon idempotency / case-sensitivity in MKT-GQL-010
gapsIdentified.rationale: ECL-1.3 covers coupon case sensitivity (user enters lowercase code). MKT-GQL-010 gets the code directly from promotionCoupons so case is always correct. Suite 077 (CPN-005) covers the end-to-end apply flow with user-typed input. Adding case-sensitivity to MKT-GQL-010 would duplicate 077 coverage at the GraphQL layer. DEFER — add a dedicated negative case only if 077 is removed or descoped.

gapsIdentified.title: MKT-GQL-005 dual-assertion: client-side vs server-side error code
gapsIdentified.rationale: The runner currently validates client-side (DV-008/DV-010) before sending, so the server never sees the missing-storeId query. If the runner is replaced, the test would need a server-side PROVIDED_NON_NULL_ARGUMENTS assertion. For now, the runner design is intentional — client-side schema validation is part of the contract. Dual-asserting would require bypassing the runner's validator which complicates maintenance.

structure.description: Header row and all 15 columns present and correctly named. All 13 IDs follow MKT-GQL-NNN pattern. No duplicate IDs. All Priority values are valid (Critical/High/Medium). CSV parses cleanly.

determinism.description: Title and Preconditions claimed DataLoader N+1 detection but the test executes sequential top-level queries — not concurrent field resolution within a single request. DataLoader batching is a within-request mechanism; sequential queries test HTTP-level response-time consistency at best. The title misrepresented the test's scope, creating an ambiguous pass/fail verdict (an agent could mark PASS based on flat times without ever exercising DataLoader).
determinism.fix: Renamed Title to 'Response-Time Consistency Across Repeated Calls'. Updated Preconditions to clarify scope: sequential queries, HTTP-level consistency, not DataLoader batching.

determinism.description: Preconditions did not clarify that after:'0' and after:'2' are numeric offset strings, not canonical cursor values. Any agent reviewing the test could misinterpret the pagination model — which is important given that GAP-001 (cursor pagination) is identified as a gap.
determinism.fix: Added clarifying note to Preconditions: 'these are numeric offset strings, not cursor values. The promotionCoupons resolver currently accepts numeric offsets in the after arg; cursor-based pagination via pageInfo.endCursor is tracked as gap GAP-001.'

determinism.description: The [MANUAL] step 'locate the promotion whose id = {{DEACTIVATED_ID}} (first item from baseline)' relies on the operator knowing the promotion's display identity from a GUID. Operators searching Admin UI typically filter by system name or display name, not internal GUID. The test captures DEACTIVATED_ID (GUID) but does not capture the systemName for easier operator lookup.
determinism.suggestedFix: Add [GQL-CAPTURE baseline.data.promotionCoupons.items.0.systemName → DEACTIVATED_SYSNAME] after the baseline capture block. Update the [MANUAL] step to: 'Admin → Marketing → Promotions → search by system name {{DEACTIVATED_SYSNAME}} (id: {{DEACTIVATED_ID}}) → open detail blade → toggle Active=false → Save.'

completeness.description: The Assertions column for after_deactivate only checks via Cross_Layer_Checks (EVIDENCE tags). There is no formal [COUNT] assertion verifying POST_TOTAL = BASELINE_TOTAL - 1 in the Assertions column itself. The assertion is buried in Cross_Layer_Checks as an EVIDENCE note, which is runner-manual verification, not an automated assertion.
completeness.suggestedFix: Add to Assertions column: '[COUNT label=after_deactivate] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount < data.promotionCoupons.totalCount (baseline) — runner evaluates {{POST_TOTAL}} < {{BASELINE_TOTAL}}' and '[DATA label=after_deactivate] {{DEACTIVATED_ID}} NOT IN after_deactivate.data.promotionCoupons.items[].id'

completeness.description: The introspection test only asserts data.__type is non-null and data.__type.fields is non-null. The EVIDENCE notes describe deeper assertions (promotionCoupons field present, storeId NON_NULL) but these are in Cross_Layer_Checks as manual evidence review, not automated assertions. A future regression that removes the promotionCoupons field from the schema would still PASS this test.
completeness.suggestedFix: Add a [DATA] assertion: '[DATA label=introspect_query] data.__type.fields[?name=="promotionCoupons"] is non-null' to make the field-presence check automated rather than evidence-only.

completeness.description: Preconditions require (a) inactive promotion, (b) private promotion, and (c) active+public+couponed promotion in the environment. This is a complex state requirement that is not guaranteed by the standard QA seed data. The Preconditions do not reference a seed data command or alias to establish this state, making the test potentially BLOCKED in fresh environments.
completeness.suggestedFix: Add to Preconditions: 'If these promotions do not exist, run /qa-seed-data marketing-coupons to establish required state. Alternatively reference @td(PROMO_INACTIVE.id) and @td(PROMO_PRIVATE.id) when aliases are added to test-data/aliases.json.'

testability.description: The EVIDENCE assertion '{{ITEM_NAME_NO_CULTURE}} should be null (or fallback) when cultureName is omitted per resolver spec' is an unfalsifiable predicate — 'null OR fallback' accepts any value. If the server returns an empty string, a wrong locale name, or a truncated value, the assertion still passes.
testability.fix: Replaced with: '{{ITEM_NAME_NO_CULTURE}} is either null or a non-empty default-locale string — resolver MUST NOT crash or return a mismatched translation; if a value is returned it must differ from the en-US localized value when distinct translations exist (verify in evidence JSON)'

testability.description: The large_first assertion '[DATA label=large_first] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount >= 0' is trivially true and adds no coverage value. totalCount is a non-negative Int by schema — it can never be < 0. A broken response would return null (caught by non-null check) or 0 (missed by this assertion).
testability.fix: Changed to: '[DATA label=large_first] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount >= 1' — the store is known to have 19+ active coupons in QA, so >= 1 is a meaningful invariant check.

dataValidity.description: ALL 13 test cases reference BL-GQL-001, BL-GQL-002, BL-GQL-004, and/or BL-AUTH-005 in Business_Rule. None of these BL-* IDs exist in business-logic.md (verified: file defines BL-PRICE-*, BL-CART-*, BL-CHK-*, BL-ORD-*, BL-AUTH-001 through BL-AUTH-006, etc. — no BL-GQL domain). The BL-GQL-* identifiers are a suite-family convention used consistently across all 050a-050i suites.
dataValidity.impact: False traceability — the Business_Rule column claims coverage of invariants that do not formally exist in the canonical reference file. This is a systematic issue across the entire 050* GraphQL suite family, not isolated to 050j.
dataValidity.suggestedFix: Option A (preferred): Promote BL-GQL-001 through BL-GQL-004 as formal entries in business-logic.md under a new Domain: GraphQL xAPI Contract. Submit as PROPOSED-BL-GQL-* for qa-lead-orchestrator approval. Option B (interim): Replace BL-GQL-* with the nearest canonical BL-* (BL-PRICE-001 for filter/data integrity, BL-AUTH-005 for auth gating). NOTE: Since 050a-050i use the same convention and are already in regression, Option A is strongly preferred to avoid mass edits. This is flagged as manual item, not auto-fixable.

dataValidity.description: Products filter hardcodes fc596540864a41bf8ab78734ee7353a3 in the products query. Per DV-013, this is an ALLOWED EXCEPTION — it is the documented B2B virtual catalog root ID from knowledge/catalog.md and knowledge/store-settings.md, which is stable across deploys.

storeId: 'STORE_DOES_NOT_EXIST' is a literal non-GUID string used as a test value for the wrong-store scenario. This is not a hardcoded entity ID — it is an intentional invalid literal for the negative test. DV-013 exception: documented test-specific literal for error injection.

The pagination offsets after:'0' and after:'2' are magic numbers with no named-constant justification. An agent reading the test cannot tell if these are arbitrary, or if 0 and 2 are significant boundary values (e.g., testing page boundary at exactly position 2 because that's where first+1 would start).
dataValidity.suggestedFix: Add a comment in Steps: '# after:0 = first page start; after:2 = exactly first:2 items into the next page — tests non-overlapping boundary'

blEclCoverage.description: See FIND-DV-001 above. BL-GQL-* IDs are not in business-logic.md. Classified here for completeness.

BL-AUTH-005 (RBAC 6-permission model) is referenced in Business_Rule for auth/security tests. The actual rule verified (no data leak, auth gating) is closer to the intent of BL-AUTH-005 but that invariant specifically covers the 6-permission Admin RBAC model — not GraphQL resolver-level auth gating. The semantic fit is approximate but not wrong.
blEclCoverage.suggestedFix: After BL-GQL-* invariants are formally promoted (see FIND-DV-001), replace BL-AUTH-005 references in these cases with BL-GQL-004 (auth gating) once that ID is canonical.

blEclCoverage.description: BL-PRICE-001 is correctly referenced for filter-correctness and admin→API cross-layer assertions. The verify clause of BL-PRICE-001 ('Verify: cart.items[].placedPrice reflects the stacked discount') is about cart pricing but the intent (promotion correctness) covers the promotionCoupons domain. Acceptable semantic reference.

ECL-2.1 (race conditions / overselling) is referenced for the anonymous auth test. The relevant ECL for auth gating on GraphQL queries is ECL-14.1 (GraphQL xAPI Error Patterns) which IS also referenced. ECL-2.1 is a less precise fit for this specific test scenario.
blEclCoverage.suggestedFix: Remove ECL-2.1 from MKT-GQL-003 Edge_Case_Refs; ECL-14.1 alone is the correct reference for anonymous GraphQL auth gating.

duplication.description: MKT-GQL-010 (E2E: Apply Returned CouponCode in Cart) and 077 CPN-005 (Copied Coupon Code — Apply to Cart via Coupons Sidebar, E2E) test the same end-to-end coupon apply flow but from different layers: MKT-GQL-010 is runner-native GraphQL mutation chaining; CPN-005 is storefront UI browser flow. Per DUP-003 this is EXPECTED cross-layer coverage — informational only.
duplication.verdict: INFORMATIONAL — expected cross-layer coverage, no action needed

duplication.description: MKT-GQL-003 (anonymous hard-gate on promotionCoupons) is conceptually similar to 050g GQL-016 (orders() hard-gate). Both test resolver-level Unauthorized patterns. However MKT-GQL-003 covers a different resolver (promotionCoupons) and is required for this suite's auth coverage. Preconditions of MKT-GQL-003 explicitly reference the 050g pattern as a precedent — this is architectural coherence, not duplication.
duplication.verdict: INFORMATIONAL — same pattern, different resolver, no action needed

duplication.description: MKT-GQL-011 (error schema / no data leak) and 050g GQL-015 (invalid queries return structured errors) both include an unknown-field probe. MKT-GQL-011 targets PromotionCouponType specifically while GQL-015 targets CustomerOrderType. Cross-layer informational — no duplication.

MKT-GQL-012 (performance: response time stability) and 050g GQL-018 (performance: happy-path response times) both measure elapsed_ms. GQL-018 covers multiple cross-cutting queries; MKT-GQL-012 is scoped to promotionCoupons. Cross-suite parallel — no duplication.
duplication.verdict: INFORMATIONAL — domain-specific vs cross-cutting, both needed

autoFixesApplied.fix: Replaced trivially-true >= 0 assertion (Int is always non-negative) with meaningful >= 1 check consistent with live-verified QA environment state (19 active coupons)

autoFixesApplied.before: [ERRORS label=large_first] errors[] empty — server caps result set without error
autoFixesApplied.after: [ERRORS label=large_first] errors[] empty — server returns full result set without error or cap
autoFixesApplied.fix: Clarified assertion text to match live-verified behavior: server does NOT cap at 10000, returns all 19 items

autoFixesApplied.before: [EVIDENCE] {{ITEM_NAME_NO_CULTURE}} should be null (or fallback) when cultureName is omitted per resolver spec
autoFixesApplied.after: [EVIDENCE] {{ITEM_NAME_NO_CULTURE}} is either null or a non-empty default-locale string — resolver MUST NOT crash or return a mismatched translation; if a value is returned it must differ from the en-US localized value when distinct translations exist (verify in evidence JSON)
autoFixesApplied.fix: Replaced unfalsifiable 'null or fallback' predicate with a structural invariant that is actually testable

autoFixesApplied.before: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Store {{STORE_ID}} has 3+ active+public couponed promotions. Two queries paginate via first:2 + after:"0" then after:"2" and the test asserts no overlap and consistent totalCount.
autoFixesApplied.after: Preconditions updated to clarify after:'0'/after:'2' are numeric offset strings (not canonical cursor values), and cross-reference GAP-001 for cursor-based pagination tracking.
autoFixesApplied.fix: Added scope clarification to prevent agent misinterpretation of pagination model

autoFixesApplied.before: Title: 'promotionCoupons — DataLoader Batch Loading (No N+1 Pattern)'; Preconditions claimed DataLoader batch loading detection
autoFixesApplied.after: Title: 'promotionCoupons — Response-Time Consistency Across Repeated Calls'; Preconditions updated to clarify scope: sequential queries, HTTP-level consistency, not DataLoader batching
autoFixesApplied.fix: Sequential top-level queries cannot detect DataLoader batching (which is within-request). Title and preconditions corrected to accurately describe what the test actually verifies.

manualItems.issue: The POST_TOTAL = BASELINE_TOTAL - 1 assertion is in Cross_Layer_Checks as an EVIDENCE note (runner-manual), not in the Assertions column as an automated assertion. A runner that skips evidence review will miss the core invariant.
manualItems.suggestedFix: Move the count decrement check from Cross_Layer_Checks to Assertions column: '[COUNT label=after_deactivate] data.promotionCoupons.totalCount = {{BASELINE_TOTAL}} - 1' and '[DATA label=after_deactivate] items[].id list does NOT contain {{DEACTIVATED_ID}}'

manualItems.issue: BL-GQL-001, BL-GQL-002, BL-GQL-004 are not defined in business-logic.md. All 13 cases reference at least one of these IDs. False traceability — test coverage matrix cannot be accurately computed.
manualItems.suggestedFix: Submit PROPOSED-BL-GQL-001 through PROPOSED-BL-GQL-004 to qa-lead-orchestrator for promotion to business-logic.md. Minimum definitions needed: BL-GQL-001 (GraphQL schema contract — queries/mutations match schema), BL-GQL-002 (connection/pagination stability — totalCount consistent, no N+1), BL-GQL-004 (resolver auth gating — protected queries return Unauthorized on anon access).

manualItems.issue: [MANUAL] step asks operator to 'locate the promotion whose id = {{DEACTIVATED_ID}}' — operators search Admin by name, not GUID.
manualItems.suggestedFix: Add [GQL-CAPTURE baseline.data.promotionCoupons.items.0.systemName → DEACTIVATED_SYSNAME] after the baseline GUID capture. Update [MANUAL] to reference both SYSNAME and ID.

manualItems.issue: Introspection test only asserts __type is non-null. A regression dropping the promotionCoupons field from the schema would not be caught by the automated assertions.
manualItems.suggestedFix: Add '[DATA label=introspect_query] data.__type.fields contains entry with name="promotionCoupons"' as an automated assertion (not just EVIDENCE).

manualItems.issue: Preconditions require (a) inactive, (b) private, (c) active+public+couponed promotions but do not reference seed data to establish this state in fresh environments.
manualItems.suggestedFix: Add reference to seed data command or @td() aliases. At minimum: 'If required state is missing, contact QA team to seed via /qa-seed-data marketing-coupons.'

manualItems.issue: ECL-2.1 (race conditions / overselling) is not a relevant edge case for an anonymous auth gate test.
manualItems.suggestedFix: Remove ECL-2.1 from Edge_Case_Refs in MKT-GQL-003. ECL-14.1 alone is sufficient.

manualItems.issue: Magic numbers after:'0' and after:'2' lack named-constant justification.
manualItems.suggestedFix: Add inline comment to Steps: '# after:0 = first page start; after:2 = non-overlapping second page boundary'

verdict.qualityGateEvaluation: Zero Blockers. Zero Criticals. 5 High findings (2 auto-fixed, 3 manual). The suite is regression-eligible after the 3 MUST-FIX manual items are addressed. Recommendation: address FIND-C-001 and FIND-DV-001 before promoting from Draft to Reviewed.
verdict.promotionRecommendation: CONDITIONAL — address MUST-FIX items first, then qa-lead-orchestrator approval

phase3NewNeededSummary: GAP-006: userId argument scoping (P1) — MKT-GQL-017 candidate
GAP-008: Expired coupon exclusion (P1) — MKT-GQL-018 candidate
